questions.text: The cook has stalled, the cash is short, and everyone in the RV is looking to you for a way out.

options.label: You rattle off five angles before the engine cools, each crazier than the last
You offer a couple of genuinely clever plays worth running
You have one idea, and you put it on the table
You wait to hear what the others come up with
You have nothing; somebody else needs to figure this out

questions.text: Someone hands you an empty warehouse and asks what an operation could look like here.

options.label: You see the whole empire already running, down to the last detail
You picture it clearly enough to describe it room by room
You can see roughly what it might become
You struggle to imagine much beyond the bare concrete
You see an empty building; dreaming it up is a waste of time

questions.text: A chemist lays out the tangled reasoning behind a new synthesis, fast and full of jargon.

options.label: You've worked out where it lands before they finish
You follow every step without trouble
You get there after a moment to think it through
You need a good chunk of it explained again
You give up; that kind of theory just loses you

questions.text: Late in the desert the talk drifts to big questions — chance, consequence, the meaning of it all.

options.label: That kind of talk bores you stiff; you'd rather get back to work
You have little patience for it, but you let it run
You'll join in if it turns to something useful
You find the ideas worth chewing on
You love turning the big mysteries over for their own sake

questions.text: The cover story has fallen apart and you need a believable new one, invented on the spot.

options.label: Inventing stories isn't in you; you'd just stick to the plain truth
You can manage a flimsy excuse, but it won't hold up
You piece together something passable with effort
You spin a decent tale when you have to
A whole convincing world pours out of you on demand

questions.text: A meeting with people you don't trust is set for dawn in the open desert.

options.label: You arrive early having planned every contingency twice over
You've thought it through and packed what you'll need
You've got the basics covered
You'll mostly improvise when you get there
You show up and hope it works out

questions.text: A single overlooked detail could bring the whole thing crashing down.

options.label: You catch the small things nobody else even notices
You're careful and rarely miss much
You watch the details that obviously matter
You skim past a lot of the fine print
Details slip right by you

questions.text: Your workspace and your books are how the whole operation stays standing.

options.label: Everything is labelled, squared away, and exactly where it belongs
You keep things tidy and well organized
It's orderly enough to find what you need
It's a bit of a jumble most days
It's pure chaos and you don't mind

questions.text: A job lands in your lap with no instructions and high stakes.

options.label: You'll make a mess of it before you make it right
Things tend to get untidy in your hands
It comes out fine, with a few loose ends
You handle it cleanly
You execute it flawlessly, nothing left dangling

questions.text: Your share of the work is dull, thankless, and entirely your responsibility.

options.label: You'll dodge it and let someone else pick up the slack
You drag your feet and do the bare minimum
You get to it eventually
You see it through because it's yours to do
You carry your load no matter what, every time

questions.text: A backyard cookout fills up with people from every corner of your life.

options.label: You're the loud center of it, working the whole yard
You get the room going and keep things lively
You join the fun once it's already rolling
You stick with one or two people off to the side
You count the minutes until you can slip out

questions.text: You're stuck waiting in a room full of strangers with time to kill.

options.label: You strike up a conversation with everyone in reach
You happily get a chat going with whoever's nearest
You'll talk if someone opens the door first
You'd rather not, but you'll answer if spoken to
You keep to yourself and say nothing

questions.text: The plan only works if one person stands up and runs the room.

options.label: You take the floor without a second thought; all eyes on you is fine
You're comfortable being the one in the spotlight
You'll do it if it's clearly the right call
You'd really rather someone else stepped up
Being the center of attention is your nightmare

questions.text: The crew is hashing out the next move and everyone's talking at once.

options.label: You hang back in the shadows and let it all play out
You mostly stay quiet on the edges
You speak up now and then
You're right in the thick of it
You drive the whole conversation from the front

questions.text: You walk into a deal with people you've never met before.

options.label: You go silent around strangers until you've sized them up
You stay pretty guarded and quiet with new faces
You're reserved at first, then loosen up
You warm to new people fairly fast
You're chatting like old friends within minutes

questions.text: Someone in the crew is clearly falling apart under the pressure.

options.label: You feel it with them and can't walk away until they're okay
You genuinely care and check that they're alright
You notice and offer a few words
You figure they'll sort themselves out
Their feelings aren't your problem

questions.text: A friend needs real help on a day you're already stretched thin.

options.label: You drop everything and give them all the time they need
You make real time for them despite the day
You help where you can fit it in
You tell them it'll have to wait
You've got your own problems; they're on their own

questions.text: A nervous newcomer is sweating through a tense first meeting.

options.label: You go out of your way to put them completely at ease
You say something to take the edge off for them
You give a small nod to settle them a little
You let them stew; that's their business
You almost enjoy watching them squirm

questions.text: Someone has wasted your time and you've had it with them.

options.label: You cut them down with the sharpest insult you've got
You let a few cruel jabs fly
You snap once, then rein it in
You bite your tongue and keep it civil
You'd never belittle someone, however angry you are

questions.text: A partner starts telling you about their life outside the work.

options.label: You tune out completely; other people's lives don't interest you
You half-listen, mostly waiting for it to end
You listen politely without really engaging
You find yourself genuinely curious
You want to know everything about what makes them tick

questions.text: A deal you can't control is unraveling by the minute.

options.label: You're instantly wound tight, nerves screaming
The stress hits you fast and hard
You feel the pressure but hold steady
You stay fairly cool through it
You barely register the strain at all

questions.text: It's the quiet hours and there's nothing left to do but wait for tomorrow.

options.label: Your mind races through everything that could go wrong
You worry over the loose ends for a long while
A few what-ifs nag at you
You let most of it go
You don't waste a second worrying

questions.text: The day swings from a windfall to a disaster and back again.

options.label: Your mood whiplashes with every turn, high to low
You swing noticeably with the ups and downs
Your mood shifts some, but you reset
You stay pretty even through it all
You're rock-steady no matter which way it breaks

questions.text: The heat is on, the law is circling, and every move carries real risk.

options.label: You stay relaxed and easy, like none of it touches you
You keep calm and loose most of the time
You're steady, with the odd jittery moment
You feel the tension building under your skin
You're permanently on edge, never able to settle

questions.text: Things have gone badly and the losses are starting to pile up.

options.label: You rarely feel down; you just push straight ahead
You get low only briefly, then shake it off
You feel it for a while before bouncing back
The gloom settles over you and lingers
It sinks you into a darkness that's hard to climb out of
